beginner_readability.detail: The corpus is generally accessible to zero-background learners. Definitions follow the bold-term + plain-English pattern required by artifact-mix.json. Acronyms (LLM, API) are spelled out on first use. The everyday analogy in section 4 (generalist employee → focused training period) effectively precedes the abstract definition. No academic hedging phrases found. Average sentence length is below 25 words throughout.

soft_findings.detail: Three new terms (Pre-trained model, Domain, Domain-specific data) are introduced consecutively in a bullet definition list. Each term has its own bold label + plain-language explanation, which is the correct formatting pattern. However, introducing all three in a single list without narrative separation is borderline for a zero-background learner. Soft finding only — the definition-list format mitigates the density. Consider adding one bridging sentence before the list.
soft_findings.escalation: no — only 1 soft finding; hard-fail threshold is 3

verdict: PASS with soft findings. All hard checks pass: required sections present and non-trivial, primary citation satisfied, ≥60% factual assertion coverage in sections 5/6/7, no fabricated citations, no scope creep, no forward-referenced or out-of-scope concepts taught at depth. Soft findings to surface at HITL #2: (1) estimated word count (~2,400–2,600) is below the standard profile floor of 3,000 but above the 30% hard-fail threshold; (2) unlisted technical terms LLM, parameters, API, and held-out test set are used with inline mitigation but absent from any known concepts_introduced list — recommend confirming they are introduced in a prior topic's corpus or adding them here; (3) three-term definition list in section 5.1 is borderline jargon_dump but formatted correctly. No builder revision required before HITL #2; findings logged for human review.
